tasks.title: Add task dependencies visualization with graph view and critical path highlighting
evidence: User request: Task Dependencies Visualization - Graph view of task dependencies showing blocking/blocked relationships with critical path highlighting
Queue already supports depends_on field for task dependencies (docs/queue-and-tasks.md)
No current visualization of dependency chains exists in CLI or TUI
Critical path analysis would help users identify which tasks are blocking the most work
plan: Research Rust graph visualization libraries (petgraph for analysis, text-based rendering or ASCII art for CLI)
Add 'ralph queue graph' subcommand for CLI dependency visualization
Implement dependency graph builder from queue.json tasks and depends_on relationships
Create graph layout algorithm for task nodes (topological sort for ordering)
Add blocking/blocked relationship display (which tasks block this task, which are blocked by it)
Implement critical path detection (longest dependency chain affecting completion)
Add highlighting for critical path tasks in graph output
Support multiple output formats: ASCII art tree, DOT format for Graphviz, JSON for external tools
Add graph view to TUI as new mode or overlay showing dependency visualization
Add filtering options: show only blocking chain for a specific task, hide completed tasks
Add cycle detection and warning for circular dependencies
Update docs/cli.md with graph command examples and output format options
Run 'make ci' to verify all changes
tasks.request: Task Dependencies Visualization - Graph view of task dependencies showing blocking/blocked relationships with critical path highlighting.

tasks.title: Add desktop notifications on task completion with optional sound alerts
evidence: User request: Notification Integration - Desktop notifications on task completion with optional sound alerts
Long-running tasks require users to monitor terminal; notifications improve UX
Cross-platform notification support needed (macOS, Linux, Windows)
Sound alerts should be optional and configurable
plan: Research Rust notification crates (notify-rust, tauri-notification, or platform-specific APIs)
Add notification config section to AgentConfig (enable/disable, sound on/off, sound file path)
Implement notify_task_complete() function in runner module
Trigger notification after successful task completion in supervise flow
Add sound alert support with platform-appropriate default sounds
Ensure notifications work on macOS (NotificationCenter), Linux (notify-send/dbus), Windows (toast)
Add CLI flag --notify/--no-notify to override config
Update docs/configuration.md with notification settings
Add tests for notification configuration parsing
Run 'make ci' to verify all changes
tasks.request: Notification Integration - Desktop notifications on task completion with optional sound alerts.

tasks.title: Create Quick Start Guide with common workflows and comparisons
evidence: User request: Quick Start Guide - Single-page getting started doc with common workflows and comparisons
Existing docs are comprehensive but scattered across multiple files: configuration.md, cli.md, workflow.md, queue-and-tasks.md
New users need a single entry point covering installation, first task, common workflows, and runner comparisons
docs/index.md currently just lists other docs without onboarding guidance
README.md has setup info but lacks workflow examples and runner comparisons
plan: Create docs/quick-start.md as a single-page guide
Include installation section (cargo install, binary download, build from source)
Cover 'ralph init' for bootstrapping a new project
Document first task workflow: ralph task -> ralph run one
Add common workflow examples: quick fix, multi-phase task, scan mode, TUI usage
Include runner comparison table (Codex, OpenCode, Gemini, Claude, Cursor) with use cases
Add phase mode comparison (1-phase vs 2-phase vs 3-phase) with when to use each
Include minimal config example to get started
Add troubleshooting section for common first-time issues
Update docs/index.md to list quick-start.md as the entry point
Update README.md to reference the Quick Start Guide
Run 'make ci' to verify docs build and links are valid
tasks.request: Quick Start Guide - Single-page getting started doc with common workflows and comparisons.

tasks.title: Add support for 'kimi' and 'pi' runners
evidence: User request: Add support for runner 'kimi' and runner 'pi'
runner_cli_inventory.sh:5 already includes kimi and pi in runner list
runner_cli_inventory.sh:447 declares RUNNERS array with kimi and pi
Existing Runner enum in config.rs:316-323 has Codex, Opencode, Gemini, Cursor, Claude
Runner binaries resolved in runner.rs:248-261
Default models defined in runner.rs:263-271
Execution functions in runners.rs implement run_<runner> and run_<runner>_resume for each runner
plan: Add Kimi and Pi variants to Runner enum in contracts/config.rs
Add kimi_bin and pi_bin fields to AgentConfig in contracts/config.rs
Update runner_label function in runner.rs to handle Kimi and Pi
Add kimi and pi binary resolution in resolve_binaries function in runner.rs
Add default models for Kimi and Pi in default_model_for_runner function
Add binary selection in run_prompt and resume_session match statements in runner.rs
Implement run_kimi, run_kimi_resume, run_pi, run_pi_resume functions in execution/runners.rs
Update CLI spec in cli_spec.rs if runner-specific options are needed
Add config defaults for kimi_bin and pi_bin in AgentConfig default
Set default approval_mode to Yolo for both runners per user request
Run 'make ci' to verify all changes compile and tests pass
tasks.request: Need a task to add support for runner 'kimi' and runner 'pi'. The runner inventory script has already been updated to output these tool inventories. Use the information from that output to onboard these runners properly. Kimi should use 'yolo' mode by default, same for pi, whatever that mode is called.

tasks.title: Optimize redaction performance - cache sensitive env vars
evidence: redaction.rs:379-395 redact_sensitive_env_values() iterates ALL env vars on every call
This is O(n*m) where n = env vars and m = redaction calls
High-output scenarios (long runner sessions) suffer performance degradation
plan: Use lazy_static or OnceLock to cache sensitive env var values at startup
Filter env vars once and store matching keys/values in static cache
Update redact_sensitive_env_values() to use cached values only
Add benchmark to measure redaction performance before/after
Ensure cache is refreshed on SIGUSR1 or similar if needed

tasks.title: Fix TUI filter cache not detecting external queue modifications
evidence: app.rs:258-264 filter cache uses queue_rev for invalidation
No mechanism to detect external queue.json modifications (another process editing)
TUI may display stale data if queue is modified externally while TUI is running
plan: Add file modification time (mtime) check for queue.json
Compare current mtime with cached mtime on each refresh
Invalidate filter cache when mtime changes
Add visual indicator in TUI when external changes detected
Consider file hash for additional consistency check if needed

tasks.title: Fix Cursor runner phase detection fragility
evidence: runners.rs:300-302 cursor_is_planning() uses string matching on '# PLANNING MODE'
Phase detection relies on prompt template content which may change
If prompt template changes, Cursor receives incorrect CLI options silently
plan: Add explicit phase metadata to PhaseInvocation struct
Pass phase information through runner execution context
Use structured phase enum (Planning/Implementation/Review) instead of string detection
Update cursor_is_planning() to use context instead of text search
Add test to ensure phase detection works correctly

tasks.title: Fix lock cleanup race condition in DirLock Drop
evidence: lock.rs:34-44 Drop impl ignores errors from remove_file and remove_dir
If lock directory contains files (race with another process), remove_dir fails silently
Orphaned lock directories may accumulate over time
plan: Add retry logic with exponential backoff for lock cleanup
Log warnings when cleanup fails for debugging
Consider using fs::remove_dir_all for force cleanup on stale locks
Add diagnostic command 'ralph doctor' to detect orphaned locks
Add test simulating concurrent lock acquisition/release

tasks.title: Enhance JSON repair for more agent-induced errors
evidence: queue.rs:96-133 attempt_json_repair only handles trailing commas and missing brackets
Cannot fix: malformed JSON strings, invalid escape sequences, type mismatches
Agents may still produce unrecoverable JSON that causes queue load failures
plan: Add repair for common string escaping errors (unescaped quotes, newlines)
Add repair for missing quotes around object keys
Add repair for single-quoted strings (convert to double-quoted)
Add logging of repair attempts for debugging
Consider using a more robust JSON repair library if available

tasks.title: Fix pre-run task update failures being silently ignored
evidence: mod.rs:307-319 pre-run update failure is logged as warning but execution continues
If update fails due to persistent issue (runner misconfiguration), task runs with stale data
User may not notice the warning in log output
plan: Add config option 'fail_on_prerun_update_error' (default: false for compatibility)
When enabled, fail fast with clear error message on pre-run update failure
Add retry logic with exponential backoff for transient failures
Improve error message to include troubleshooting steps
Log the full error to debug log for investigation

tasks.title: Fix atomic write temp file leak on persist failure
evidence: fsutil.rs:192-211 write_atomic() may leak temp file if persist() fails after write
NamedTempFile::persist() error path doesn't explicitly clean up
Temp file accumulation in target directory over time
plan: Use NamedTempFile::close() or let it drop to auto-cleanup on error
Wrap persist in match statement with explicit cleanup on Err
Add periodic cleanup of orphaned temp files in ralph_temp_root()
Add test verifying no temp files remain after failed writes

tasks.title: Add config validation for instruction_files existence
evidence: config.rs:127-204 validate_config() doesn't check instruction_files paths exist
Missing instruction files only discovered at runtime during prompt rendering
User gets late error instead of early validation feedback
plan: Add validation loop for instruction_files in validate_config()
Check each path exists and is readable
Return helpful error with file path and suggestion to create or fix path
Add --validate-config flag to ralph config command for early checking
Add test for missing instruction file detection

tasks.title: Improve runner process timeout handling race condition
evidence: process.rs:86-170 wait_for_child() has race between timeout detection and process exit
Process exiting between interrupt and kill may have exit status misinterpreted
Lines 155-162: status checked after timeout but timeout error takes precedence
plan: Refactor to use select/poll for proper timeout handling
Check exit status immediately after interrupt before timeout error
Add state machine for process lifecycle (Running -> Interrupting -> Killed)
Add test with simulated slow-exiting process
Log process state transitions for debugging

tasks.title: Add TUI help text for all key bindings and commands
evidence: TUI has many key bindings but no built-in help overlay showing all options
Users must refer to external docs or discover keys by trial
Help overlay exists but may not cover all available actions
plan: Audit all key bindings in tui/events/ module
Create comprehensive help overlay showing all bindings by category
Add '?' key to toggle help overlay from any mode
Include command palette shortcuts in help
Update docs/cli.md with TUI key reference

tasks.title: Add LFS smudge/clean filter validation before commit
evidence: gitutil.rs:250-274 LFS detection only checks tracking files, not filter health
Doesn't handle LFS pointer file corruption or smudge/clean filter failures
Potential data loss if LFS files committed as pointers due to filter misconfiguration
plan: Add pre-commit LFS filter validation check
Run 'git lfs status' to detect files that should be LFS but aren't
Warn user if LFS filters are not properly configured
Add option to abort commit on LFS misconfiguration
Add test with mock LFS repository

tasks.title: Fix debug log test interference from concurrent access
evidence: debuglog.rs:55-70 enable() silently succeeds if already enabled
reset_for_tests() requires test lock but tests may not always acquire it
If tests run concurrently without lock, they may interfere with debug log state
plan: Add test-only mutex guard around all debug log operations
Use thread-local storage for test isolation where possible
Add panic if debug log state changes unexpectedly during tests
Document test concurrency requirements in module docs
Add test verifying concurrent debug log access is safe

tasks.title: Add CLI completion scripts for bash/zsh/fish
evidence: Ralph has many CLI commands and flags but no shell completion support
Users must type full command names and remember all flags
Clap supports generating completion scripts via clap_complete crate
plan: Add clap_complete dependency
Create 'ralph completions <shell>' command
Generate completions for bash, zsh, fish, and PowerShell
Add installation instructions to README and docs/cli.md
Consider including pre-generated scripts in repo
